text.title: graph-rag-retrieval Failure Modes
text.summary: Four observable antipatterns when applying graph-rag-retrieval, each with a detector signal and a concrete repair.

antipattern.description: Returning a coarse community summary as the answer for ENTITY or RELATIONSHIP queries instead of routing to neighbor lookup or path traversal.
antipattern.reason: Community summaries blur multiple entities together. Symptom: answers feel "about the right topic" but never name the specific relationship the user asked about. Detector: `retrieval_path=summary` for `query_type IN (ENTITY,RELATIONSHIP)`. Fix: in router, force ENTITY → entity-neighbors and RELATIONSHIP → shortest-path; only LOCAL can fall back to vector after entity miss.

antipattern.description: Running full live graph traversal at query time on graphs >50k nodes to answer GLOBAL questions.
antipattern.reason: Latency spikes from sub-second to tens of seconds. Detector: p95 latency for `query_type=GLOBAL` > 3s. Fix: pre-compute hierarchical/community summaries offline (graph-rag-indexing) and serve GLOBAL from the dict — never traverse.

antipattern.description: Expanding to every neighbor of every entity, no cap. Context window fills with low-relevance entity chunks.
antipattern.reason: Faithfulness drops because the generator anchors on noisy expanded context. Detector: assembled_context length spikes 5-10x baseline. Fix: cap at 3 neighbors per entity (r4) and dedupe by chunk_id before assembling.

antipattern.description: Calling `nx.shortest_path` without try/except; uncaught NetworkXNoPath or NodeNotFound crashes the retrieval step for valid relationship queries.
antipattern.reason: Disconnected components are common in real graphs. Detector: 5xx rate on `retrieval_path=shortest-path` > 0. Fix: wrap every shortest_path call (r3), catch NetworkXNoPath + NodeNotFound, then trigger LOCAL fallback (r5) instead of failing the request.
